question: Choose the sentence that correctly uses the phrase 'by accident':
choices: She revealed the ending by accident — she truly didn't mean to
He told the ending by accident on purpose last night
They arrived by accident to the concert on the wrong day
The director made the film by accident without planning it

question: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'in all likelihood':
choices: She was very excited, so she left in all likelihood
He studied well, but in all likelihood forgot his textbook
In all likelihood, the show will be cancelled if it keeps losing viewers
The audience was in all likelihood expecting something better

question: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'on purpose':
choices: She didn't mean to spoil the film, but she did it on purpose
He planned the surprise party on purpose to make her happy
On purpose, nobody arrived at the cinema on time that evening
The director made mistakes on purpose without any reason

question: Which of the following correctly uses 'Supposing we...' to make a suggestion?
choices: Supposing we have changed the meeting time to 3 pm?
Supposing we changed the meeting time to 3 pm?
Supposing we will change the meeting time to 3 pm?
Supposing we to change the meeting time to 3 pm?

question: Which of the following correctly uses 'What if...' to make a suggestion?
choices: What if we discussed the problem after the meeting instead?
What if we will discuss the problem after the meeting instead?
What if we have discussed the problem after the meeting?
What if we are discussing the problem after the meeting?

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'It's possible that the map wasn't the original — it might have been a copy made later.' What does 'might not have been' express here?
choices: A positive deduction — the speaker is certain
A negative certainty — the speaker is absolutely sure it was not true
Less certainty — the speaker thinks it is possible it was not the original
A logical conclusion based on clear evidence

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'The platform was high up and _____ easy for people to reach, although it was only a kilometre from the centre of Petra.'
choices: can't have been
must have been
might have been
would have been

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'He _____ lying; he's totally untrustworthy and never tells the truth.'
choices: can't have been
would have been
might have been
must have been

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'She _____ revising because she failed her exam — and she had been out all evening.'
choices: must have been
would have been
couldn't have been
might have been

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'There _____ much simpler ways of knitting gloves in the past — the technology was very basic.'
choices: must be
can't be
might be
would have been

question: Choose the correct reduced form: 'After I had been to the gym, I showered and had some lunch.'
choices: After having been to the gym, I showered and had some lunch
Having been at the gym, I showered and had some lunch
Been to the gym, I showered and had some lunch
Being to the gym, I showered and had some lunch

question: Choose the correct reduced form: 'I knew what had happened. I wasn't surprised.'
choices: Having known what had happened, I was not surprised
Not knowing what had happened, I was not surprised
To know what had happened, I wasn't surprised
Knowing what had happened, I wasn't surprised

question: What does the reduced clause 'Not realising what time it was, I missed the train' show?
choices: The subject was aware of the time but chose to ignore it
The subject arrived at the station exactly on time
The subject's lack of awareness of the time caused a result
The result happened before the subject left the station

question: What is the function of the participle clause in: 'Like many people who watched the film, I left the cinema wanting to know more about the main star.'
choices: It shows that the two actions happened one after another in sequence
It shows the result of leaving the cinema
It shows that two things happened at the same time
It shows that wanting happened before leaving the cinema

question: What is the function of the participle clause in: 'After reading about his life growing up in London, I realised his childhood was similar to the boy in the film.'
choices: Showing two things happening at the same time
Showing the result of reading about his life
Showing a sequence of events — reading came before realising
Showing a negative action and its consequence

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'The new ________ play is set in ancient Greece and features famous historical figures.'
choices: historical
historic
classic
comical

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'Today was a ________ day for the world's largest movie studio.'
choices: comical
historical
classic
historic

question: The adjective 'appalling' describes a performance that is ________.
choices: very funny and makes the audience laugh out loud
full of energy and keeps everyone's attention throughout
far too simple and lacking any depth or creativity
shockingly bad in quality

question: Choose the correct evaluative adjective: 'Her acting was totally ________; I couldn't believe in her as a character at all.'
choices: exceptional
offensive
unconvincing
lively

question: Choose the correct word to complete: 'We say comedians ________ when they tell jokes informally.'
choices: deliver the plot
crack a few jokes
heckle the audience
catch up on the cast

question: Which of the following words describes a colour or design that is dull and uninteresting?
choices: vibrant
distinctive
insipid
rich

question: Which of the following describes a positive quality of art or colour?
choices: subdued
jarring
clashing
vibrant

question: What do news sites usually do to warn readers about spoilers?
choices: They completely hide the ending of the film from all readers
They refuse to publish any reviews that might contain spoilers
They signal that there are spoilers in their reviews
They only publish reviews after the film has been removed from cinemas

question: What reason does the author give for re-reading books we love?
choices: To practice our reading speed and improve our comprehension
Because we cannot remember what happened the first time we read
Because we want to tell other people what the book is about
Not to discover what happens, but because we enjoy the experience itself

question: According to information about Jordan's parliament, who appoints the Prime Minister of Jordan?
choices: The members of the House of Deputies
The members of the Senate
The King
The people in a general election

question: According to information about Jordan's parliament, how are the members of the Senate chosen?
choices: They are elected by the people every four years
They are given four-year terms by the King
They are appointed by the Prime Minister
They are selected by the House of Deputies

question: According to information about Jordan's parliament, 12 of the 138 seats are reserved for ________.
choices: women
minorities
young politicians
independent candidates

question: According to information about Jordan's parliament, the 'legislation' is defined as ________.
choices: the important ministers who control government policy
the principles and ideas by which a country is governed
the first version of a proposed law
the process of making laws

question: An important strategy for resilience is to 'see a setback or disappointment as ________.'
choices: a reason to give up and try a different path in life
an opportunity to learn something about yourself or about life
proof that you are not suited to the challenge you are facing
a warning that you need to change your personality

question: According to the resilience article, which of the following is a key strategy for building resilience?
choices: Avoid social contact when feeling stressed or overwhelmed
Never share problems with friends or family to stay strong
Pretend that difficulties do not exist and focus only on fun
Make time for activities that you enjoy and that bring you pleasure

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', where does the owner of the woods live?
choices: In the woods, in a small cabin hidden among the trees
Near the frozen lake on the edge of the forest
In the village
In a farmhouse near the edge of the forest

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', why does the speaker's horse shake its harness bells?
choices: To signal to the owner of the woods that someone has stopped
Because it is cold and shivering in the winter snow
To ask whether stopping in this unusual place is a mistake
To warn the speaker that they are on someone else's land

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', the word 'must' in 'My little horse must think it queer' expresses ________.
choices: an obligation — the horse is required to think this
the speaker's certainty about what the horse is thinking
permission — the horse is allowed to think this
a polite request directed towards the horse

question: The poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' was part of a collection called ________.
choices: The Road Not Taken
Birches
New Hampshire
A Boy's Will

question: In the article about article writing, in order to 'summarise the plot', you could use the phrase ________.
choices: 'Far from being... I believe...'
'The series tells the story of...'
'I was blown away by the performance of...'
'I would argue that...'

question: In the context of avoiding repetition in writing, which of the following is a synonym for 'story'?
choices: cast
episode
set
plot

question: Which of the following CORRECTLY states the idea in the article on watching box sets?
choices: 'Far from being mindless entertainment, TV can be the most exciting narrative art form we have'
Watching box sets is always a waste of time for young people
Young people should only watch serious documentaries rather than box sets
The longer format of TV means topics cannot be explored in any depth

question: In grammar, 'we use would + have + past participle' in order to ________.
choices: express a polite request in the present
make an assumption about the past or draw a logical conclusion
describe something that is happening right now
give instructions about how to do something step by step

question: Which of the following correctly forms the past modal continuous?
choices: modal + have + past participle
modal + be + present participle
modal + have been + present participle
modal + have been + past participle

question: Which of the following correctly forms the passive past modal?
choices: modal + have + past participle
modal + have been + past participle
modal + be + past participle
modal + have been + present participle
